seoTitle: Gooseberry Hill vs Gorrie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gooseberry Hill and Gorrie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gooseberry Hill vs Gorrie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gooseberry Hill and Gorrie.
heading: Gooseberry Hill vs Gorrie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gooseberry Hill, Western Australia and Gorrie, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gorrie has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Gooseberry Hill (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gooseberry Hill or Gorrie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gooseberry Hill has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Gorrie scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gooseberry Hill scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Gorrie scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gooseberry Hill or Gorrie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gorrie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
